Iksar Hide Manual  
 

Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 5
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
INT: +5 MANA: +45
SV DISEASE: +10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except WAR MNK ROG BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

where in CHardok
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2001 at 9:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762314253
14 posts
I have asked many people and no one seems to know.....Where in Chardok is this Mob found?
▲ Page top
RE: where in CHardok
QuoteReply
# Feb 25 2002 at 12:31 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592251309369
Scholar
25 posts
Just using deduction...where do think an Auditor would be found? Probably close to money...so I would guess the bank.

But, all deductions aside...I got mine from the bank area. ;-)
▲ Page top
All around good item -
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 6:50 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756508315
View User Forum
70 posts
Just the fact that it has AC and resists as well as 45 mana ( 5 wis) and goes in the range is a great item. If it wasnt range equipable I could only see Mages and Enchanters really getting the benifite of it.
Tonight we where down by the bank area and a mob droped this just before about 14 mobs jumped us. With 2 groups we held it for about 5 min then died. =\ Enchanter got the book but ever one had a great time. Seems chardok has taken a turn for the better both in EXP/Loots and fun :)
Happy hunting all...
▲ Page top
Iksar eh?
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 4:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249026364
54 posts
Hrmm, ya know I was thinking. Wouldn't it be cool to be an iksar, and collect a piece for every slot? You'd be like...2 iksars in one! Stay with me here. You could go around wearing your suit of iksar hide, and say "this is a family heirloom, you see, my great uncle bernie had this terrbile accident with a skinning knife and we discovered just how tough a ******* he really was so we decided to wear him. It's the latest fashion wearing your relatives.."

I dunno, I just think it's funny. Kind of like an erudite running around with some other erudites heart around their neck...where did it come from? What vile fiend would do such a thing to one of their own race? And more importantly, whose heart are you giving someone when you hand them yours? Is it your heart? or is it your cousan selma's heart in your possesion...

these are the kinds of things you think about as a cleric....in seb...when there's 49 people in the zone, and 7 of them are clerics...

Shaklor Soulraizer
Pushin 55 for a long time but just can't seem to do it.
▲ Page top
RE: Iksar eh?
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2001 at 4:03 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767675099
136 posts
its probably not YOUR heart or you would die after trading it to someone.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 12 2001 at 10:22 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) another book
Wis Casters
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 7:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250847488
Guru
237 posts
Hmm, as a Wis caster myself, I still find this item rather attractive to have. Yes, the intelligence is not really needed, but the resists aren't all that bad, and since I'm also trying to advance my trade skills, the extra int doesn't hurt me.
▲ Page top
RE: Wis Casters
QuoteReply
# Oct 13 2001 at 5:42 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361479811
54 posts
Trade skills are figured of the highest stat from wis or int, but still. the resists and mana are nice to have
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 14 2001 at 9:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Nothing has been confirmed as to helping or subtracting other then skill and trivial level. And don't go saying so in so site said blah blah blah. I've seen it first hand.
RE: Wis Casters
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 1:42 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249550698
View User Forum
98 posts
No one said anything specific on how it helped tradeskills. He merely stated that the higher of either WIS or INT was the factor that determined a positive effect on tradeskills. And if you were saying that the WIS/INT thing is un-confirmed, I quite clearly remember reading a tradeskill related post on the Developer's Corner Board on everquest.com (the board only VI and Sony Reps can post to). There they stated the highest of your INT or WIS help with your tradeskill development.
▲ Page top
name...
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 7:25 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763043733
107 posts
Anyone else feel like verant has a hard time coming up with names for the new stuff, iksar hide manual? wtf :(
▲ Page top
RE: name...
QuoteReply
# Oct 13 2001 at 3:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248874660
137 posts
yeah Elven skin manual would be much better. but then sarnak seem to have something against us iksar, the same way i have something against elves.

P.S.: If you try to get Somethingslayer as your lastname its always rejected at least it did that for me stupid verant dont want an Iksar named Daakar Elfslayer. to bad that would rock dont you think.
▲ Page top
RE: name...
QuoteReply
# Oct 13 2001 at 1:13 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Actually that surname problem is because the name filter used for surnames is the same as the one used for first names. Since ????slayer isn't an acceptable first name most of the variations have been put into the filter. You can have that surname, you just have to petition and ask for the name to be manually assigned to you.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 13 2001 at 1:28 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The name will STILL be rejected and if you ever get caught with it if you DO get it, they will change it. So and so the elf slayer is considred a title and you only get titles from winning tournaments. I have seen a lot of people on Rallos come up with a cool name, petition something not thinking about it and get their name changed.
#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 24 2001 at 12:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I know someone whose surname is Gnomeslayer, and I'm certain that it's acceptable because it was changed by a GM from Nastywind. So ___slayer is fine, but THE ___slayer is a title and unacceptable.

Am I the only one noticing a lack of +wis on this item even though it can be used by wis casters and hybrids? seems silly. go VI.

Actually, some WIS casters wouldn't mind the lack of WIS on this item. It has mana, great if you are already at 200 WIS (or INT for that matter) and 10 SvPoison - good for Trakanon raids.

__DEL__1593965790794

He's probably a Paladin (like myself). I too noticed only INT and went BAH...until I noticed the +45 MANA...well worth it..we don't need no stinkin' badg..err..wis bonus!
▲ Page top
Yeah Yeah
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 2:53 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592251734630
1 post
This is a pretty Sweet Item. After all if you think about it, get 3 of these books since they're not lore, and you have 15ac 30 dis/poi 15int and 135mana sounds nice if you're an int caster going after Trak.
▲ Page top
RE: Yeah Yeah
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 8:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1624235044287
192 posts
Actually, if you were going for the PR for trak, get two of these and a stein of mogguk. Stein has 25 save poison, plus some other stuff (and a stein is a LOT easier to get)

Altogether, two of these and a stein: 10ac, 45 poison, 20 disease, 20int, 90mana, 5dex, 10hp.

For a Trak raid, that 45 save poison would be VERY nice. Heck, for most any situation, that would be a nice setup. I know, at the levels you can get this a caster should already have 200int, but they do have to get it from some slot. There are items that are superior to the stein, but the setup of a stein and two of these would be absolutely great against trak (that's the point I'm trying to make.) Lowest base PR I've seen for any class/race is 15 I believe, so with this setup, you've got 60 PR, plus the rest of your slots to add more poison gear.

Ok, enough of my banter, and please don't flame me if I didn't make sense (because I'm not sure if I did)

*edit: I guess stein of mogguk gives 25 save disease, so that combo doesn't work quite as well. Still, that's 45 save disease for those times you need some beefy disease resist (not sure what those would be)*
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
